definition: For pair (0,1), set A=A_w(x), S=sum_i x_i^2, N=x0*d0A-x1*d1A. The pairwise barrier Schur inequality is equivalent to P_w(x)>=0 on the barrier region 24^(w/2)A_w(x)>=A_w(1)S^(w/2), where P_w=-(x0-x1)(S*N-w*A*(x0^2-x1^2)).
name: Global pairwise Schur polynomial target
next_certificate_target: Find an exact Positivstellensatz/SOS/Krawtchouk certificate proving P_w>=0 on {24^(w/2)A_w - A_w(1)S^(w/2)>=0}.
positive_denominators_removed: The sign of theta0-theta1 matches the sign of x0-x1, and A,S>0 inside the positive orthant.
why_not_finished: P_w has negative coefficients and can be negative below the barrier. The needed certificate is semialgebraic: P_w>=0 under the barrier constraint, not coefficientwise positivity.
why_this_is_exact: P_w is an integer sparse polynomial obtained directly from the Golay shell monomial polynomial.
